questions.q: Usha Mehta is best remembered for operating which clandestine facility during the freedom struggle?
options: A printing press for banned books
questions.explanation: Usha Mehta ran the secret Congress Radio, an underground broadcasting station, during the 1942 Quit India movement.

questions.q: The secret radio station Usha Mehta helped run was associated with which 1942 mass movement?
questions.explanation: The Congress Radio broadcast nationalist messages during the Quit India Movement launched in August 1942.

questions.q: What was the name of the secret radio station that Usha Mehta helped operate?
questions.explanation: The underground station she ran was known as the Congress Radio (also called the Secret Congress Radio).

questions.q: In which year did Usha Mehta operate the secret Congress Radio?
questions.explanation: The Congress Radio began broadcasting in 1942 in the wake of the Quit India movement.

questions.q: What happened to Usha Mehta after the British authorities tracked down the secret radio?
questions.explanation: Usha Mehta was arrested in November 1942 and imprisoned for her role in running the underground station.

questions.q: In which Indian city was the secret Congress Radio chiefly operated by Usha Mehta and her associates?
questions.explanation: The Congress Radio was operated from various locations in Bombay (present-day Mumbai).

questions.q: What was the main purpose of the secret radio station Usha Mehta ran?
options: To broadcast nationalist news and messages
questions.explanation: The station broadcast nationalist news and messages to counter the British-controlled official media during Quit India.

questions.q: Usha Mehta is generally classified among which group of freedom fighters?
options: Women activists of the Quit India era
questions.explanation: Usha Mehta was a young woman activist who played a prominent role during the 1942 Quit India movement.

questions.q: What kind of medium did Usha Mehta use to spread nationalist messages in 1942?
questions.explanation: She used radio broadcasting through the underground Congress Radio to reach listeners across India.

questions.q: The Congress Radio that Usha Mehta ran was described as which kind of station?
questions.explanation: It was a secret, underground, unlicensed station that operated in defiance of British broadcasting controls.

questions.q: Roughly when did the secret Congress Radio that Usha Mehta operated begin its broadcasts?
questions.explanation: The Congress Radio began transmitting in August 1942 (its first broadcast was on 14 August 1942), days after the Quit India resolution was passed.

questions.q: In which month and year was Usha Mehta arrested for running the secret radio?
questions.explanation: British authorities traced and shut down the station, arresting Usha Mehta in November 1942.

questions.q: How long was Usha Mehta imprisoned following her arrest over the Congress Radio?
questions.explanation: Usha Mehta was sentenced and remained in prison for roughly four years, until 1946.

questions.q: Usha Mehta's first brush with the freedom struggle came as a child during which earlier campaign?
options: Gandhi's civil disobedience and salt agitation era
questions.explanation: As a girl she was drawn to the movement during Gandhi's civil disobedience years of the early 1930s.

questions.q: The Congress Radio operated by Usha Mehta frequently relayed messages and statements from which kind of leaders?
questions.explanation: It broadcast guidance and statements from Congress leaders who had gone underground after the August 1942 arrests.

questions.q: What major challenge did Usha Mehta and her colleagues face while running the secret radio?
options: Constantly shifting locations to avoid detection
questions.explanation: To evade the British wireless detection units, the operators repeatedly changed transmitter locations.

questions.q: Which technical collaborator is associated with building and running the transmitter of the Congress Radio that Usha Mehta voiced?
questions.explanation: Radio technician Bob Tanna assembled and operated the transmitter that Usha Mehta and her group used.

questions.q: In later life, Usha Mehta became distinguished in which profession?
questions.explanation: After independence Usha Mehta became a respected academic, teaching and writing on civics and Gandhian thought.

questions.q: The Quit India movement, the backdrop to Usha Mehta's radio work, was launched with which famous Gandhian call?
questions.explanation: Gandhi launched Quit India in August 1942 with the call 'Do or Die,' the movement during which Usha Mehta ran her radio.

questions.q: What recurring element typically opened the broadcasts of the Congress Radio that Usha Mehta ran?
options: A reading of British war news
An announcement of its wavelength and a nationalist message
questions.explanation: Broadcasts opened by announcing the station's frequency and carrying patriotic news and messages for listeners across India.

questions.q: Usha Mehta was born in 1920 in a village in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Usha Mehta was born on 25 March 1920 in Saras, a village in the Surat district of Gujarat.

questions.q: The Congress Radio operated by Usha Mehta is often noted for announcing itself broadcasting from a wavelength of approximately what figure?
questions.explanation: The station famously identified itself as broadcasting on a wavelength of about 42.34 metres.

questions.q: Which civil servant or official is associated with the police operation that finally located and shut down the Congress Radio in 1942?
options: British wireless detection and police of Bombay
The Indian Civil Service in Delhi
questions.explanation: Bombay police, aided by British wireless direction-finding units, traced and raided the transmitter in November 1942.

questions.q: Apart from Usha Mehta, the secret radio team included several young associates. What was the group's broad political affiliation?
questions.explanation: The group consisted of young Congress workers and student volunteers committed to the Quit India cause.

questions.q: In recognition of her life's work, Usha Mehta was conferred which national honour in 1998?
questions.explanation: Usha Mehta was awarded the Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian honour, in 1998.

questions.q: Usha Mehta reportedly took part in her first protest as a child of about eight years, demonstrating against which symbol of colonial authority?
questions.explanation: As a young child she joined demonstrations against the Simon Commission, which toured India in 1928.

questions.q: After her release from prison, in which decade did Usha Mehta primarily build her academic career in Bombay?
questions.explanation: Following her release in 1946, she pursued higher studies and from the late 1940s built a long academic career in Bombay.

questions.q: The Congress Radio operated by Usha Mehta is frequently cited as significant because it did what during a period of severe repression?
options: Kept nationalist communication alive when leaders were jailed
questions.explanation: With most Congress leaders imprisoned after August 1942, the secret radio kept nationalist communication and morale alive.

questions.q: Usha Mehta's academic and public writing in later years focused most strongly on which thinker's ideas?
questions.explanation: She became a noted exponent of Gandhian thought, writing and lecturing extensively on Gandhi's ideas.

questions.q: In which year did Usha Mehta pass away?
questions.explanation: Usha Mehta died on 11 August 2000 in Mumbai.
